title: acer italum subsp. opulifolium
intro: Acer italum subsp. opulifolium, also known as the Italian maple or Opulent-leaved maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to the mountainous regions of Italy. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce fever and inflammation due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Acer italum subsp. opulifolium include its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
